tags.description: Basalt islet, commissioned as HMS Diamond Rock February 1804 (Royal Navy). Wall drops to 40m. Cathedral-shaped crack through islet accessible from ~16m. Hawksbill turtles congregate at base. Gorgonian fans, soft corals, sponges on walls. Moray eels, barracuda, jacks, reef sharks in channel currents. Strong variable currents — Advanced only.

Triangular cavern formation at base of Diamond Rock islet. Accessible from ~16m depth. Sea fans, soft corals, sponges inside. Moray eels, crustaceans, glassy sweepers, hawksbill turtles. Light beams filter through triangular opening. French angelfish, parrotfish, spiny lobsters in volcanic rock crevices.

Sheltered black-sand cove near Les Anses d'Arlet. 0-25m range; primary diving 5-12m. Green turtles and hawksbill turtles confirmed and abundant — daily sightings. Parrotfish, damselfish, trumpetfish on reef. Ctenophores documented. Nurse sharks NOT confirmed at this site.
tags.correction_note: Removed nurse shark claim — not confirmed by alphaplongee.com operator page or snorkeling-report.com

tags.description: At least 8 diveable wrecks in Saint-Pierre Bay, all sunk 8 May 1902 by Mont Pelée pyroclastic surge. Depths range 12-60m (Amélie shallowest ~12m; Tamaya deepest ~85m though beyond recreational limits). Barracudas, snappers, trumpetfish, lionfish, sponges, soft corals confirmed across wreck sites.

Remote volcanic islet in Réserve Marine du Prêcheur, northwest Martinique. Walls alternate with coral plateaux, arches, submarine faults from shallows to 40m+. Dominica Channel current. Horse-eye jacks, barracuda, snappers, parrotfish in large schools confirmed. Sea turtles regular. Protected marine reserve keeps fish populations dense.

Black volcanic sand beach adjacent to Anse Dufour. Rocky reef to 8m. Sea turtles common, parrotfish, wrasse, damselfish, juvenile angelfish. Octopuses in crevices — good site for cephalopods. Cleaning stations on volcanic rock.

Canyon and drop-off dive north of Case-Pilote. Canyons, caves, natural arches 0-25m. Large feather gorgonians on canyon walls confirmed. Moray eels, angelfish, stonefish, turtles, lobsters, octopuses confirmed. Afternoon dives preferred by local operators — fish activity highest. Good for drift diving.
Basaltic headland reef near Les Anses d'Arlet. 3-12m plateau. Parrotfish, surgeonfish, stingrays, sea turtles confirmed. Strong currents attract turtles. Beginner-friendly.

Three-masted sailing ship (converted buoy tender), built 1911 Royan France, original name 'Quinette de Richemond'. Renamed Nahoon 1967. Used in North Sea, Gironde estuary, Madagascar, Caribbean. Abandoned Fort de France Bay 1984. Deliberately sunk 1993 off Anse Dufour/Cap Salomon as artificial reef. 35m depth, ~35m long. Steering wheel preserved and mobile. Engine room and holds accessible. Barracuda, jacks, moray eels, stingrays, snappers, lobsters, stonefish, Spanish mackerel, sponges confirmed. CMAS 2/Advanced minimum.
tags.correction_note: Previous record said 'three-masted schooner built in 1911'. Multiple sources confirm it was a buoy and beacon maintenance vessel converted, not originally a schooner. Sinking date 1993 confirmed.

tags.description: Fringing reef in front of Grande Anse d'Arlet bay (iconic church-and-bay scenery). 0-14m. Sea turtles reliable — same population as adjacent Anse Dufour/Anse Noire sites. Parrotfish, angelfish, blue tangs, stingrays on sandy floor.

tags.qa_note: Near Le Prêcheur/northwest Martinique based on coordinates and source listing. No site-specific dive documentation found beyond general operator listing. Marine life based on regional fauna.

tags.description: Shore reef 5-10m near Les Anses d'Arlet. Coral-garden layout of rocky outcrops with sandy channels. Seahorses reported. Parrotfish, damselfish, wrasse, French and grey angelfish, sea turtles. Good macro and species ID site.
tags.qa_note: Seahorse claim from prior record — consistent with regional operator listings but not confirmed at this specific site by a second independent source. Noted as reported.

tags.name: Site de Plongée La Perle
tags.description: Îlet de la Perle, less than 2 miles offshore in Dominica Channel. Walls from 20-25m to 40m+. Strong open-ocean currents. Barracudas, horse-eye jacks consistently reported. Groupers, snappers, parrotfish on reef. Hawksbill turtles on wall faces. Marlins occasionally sighted in channel. Experienced drift diving skills required.

tags.seamark:name: Krystal Toy
tags.qa_note: UNRESOLVED: No independent dive guide documentation found for Krystal Toy. OSM tags vessel name and hull_showing category. No vessel history, sinking date, or site-specific marine life found from any source. Previous perplexity-generated description removed as unverified. Requires local Martinique operator contact.

tags.name: Le Roraima (épave)
tags.description: 103.63m × 11.64m iron-hulled steamship, 2,712 gross tons. Built June 5 1883 at Aitken & Mansel, Whiteinch, Glasgow for English Mogul Line (launched as 'Roraima'). Purchased 1900 by Quebec Steamship Company. Anchored in Saint-Pierre bay when Mont Pelée erupted 8 May 1902. Ship caught fire and burned for 3 days before sinking. 68 aboard, 11 survived (including Clara King and Marguerite Hamilton Stokes). Bow at 36m, stern at 55m. Barracudas, snappers, trumpetfish, lionfish, reef fish on 120+ year-old encrusted hull. Jacques Cousteau nicknamed it 'The White-haired Wreck' for hair-like algae. CMAS 3 / Advanced with Deep+Wreck specialties required.

tags.name: Épave Amelie
tags.description: 48m iron sailing vessel built 1890 at Sevestre shipyard, Chantenay. Arrived Saint-Pierre from Marseille April 1902. Developed hull leak; towed to Anse Turin (Caret) for repairs, sank before Mont Pelée eruption of 8 May 1902. Now scattered in pieces on grey volcanic sand at 5-8m. Four yellow boundary buoys. Shore entry from Anse Turin. Sponges, hard corals, gorgonians, worms, anemones on hull. Sergeant major fish, smooth trunkfish, dusky squirrelfish, occasional longlure frogfish, rays, turtles confirmed. Sandy areas host torpedo rays.

tags.name: Le Banc de Sable
tags.description: Sandy bank with patch reef near Le Prêcheur on Martinique's northern Atlantic coast. 8-12m. Sandy clearings with coral formations. Stingrays on sandy bottom, reef fish in coral patches. Best dived early morning before Atlantic conditions build.
tags.correction_note: Removed previous perplexity-generated description claiming rays/garden eels/20-25m visibility without source basis.

tags.name: Banc du Singe
tags.description: Sand reef south of Sainte-Luce. 5-18m. Intermediate site with light to moderate current. No site-specific marine life reports found; French Antilles broader Caribbean fauna applies.
tags.correction_note: Removed previous perplexity-generated description claiming rays, garden eels and specific conditions without source basis.

tags.name: Caye Duprey
tags.description: Coral reef near Sainte-Luce southern coast. 8-20m. Hard coral formations and sponges. No site-specific marine life reports found; French Antilles broader Caribbean fauna applies.
tags.correction_note: Removed previous perplexity-generated description claiming rays, turtles without source basis.

tags.name: Banc du Mitan
tags.description: Shallow coral reef plateau near Sainte-Luce. 8-15m. 'Mitan' is Creole for 'middle', reflecting mid-channel position. Good beginner site with calm conditions. No site-specific marine life reports found; French Antilles broader Caribbean fauna applies.

tags.name: Banc Major
tags.description: Major coral bank south of Sainte-Luce. 10-20m. Hard coral and gorgonian formations on outer face. No site-specific marine life reports found; French Antilles broader Caribbean fauna applies.

tags.name: Grand-Caye
tags.description: Large cay reef on Martinique south coast between Sainte-Luce and Rivière-Pilote. 6-18m. Broad coral platform. No site-specific marine life reports found; French Antilles broader Caribbean fauna applies.

tags.name: Baignoire de Joséphine
tags.description: Famous sandbank ~2km off Le François, Atlantic coast. 60cm-1.2m depth (tide-dependent). Named after Empress Joséphine de Beauharnais who was born in Martinique. Snorkelling/wading destination not scuba site. Seagrass beds with herbivorous juvenile fish. Boat/catamaran access only.
